examples: Defense Department computer network called ARPAnet, which stood for Advanced Research Projects Agency Network.
The Internet had its origins in a 1969 U.S. Defense Department computer network called ARPAnet, which stood for Advanced Research Project Agency.
The same year, Arpanet ceased to exist.
Anonymity was built into the Internet's design from the days of its progenitor (Arpanet) in the 1960s.
This means he and his buddies were sprigs in their 20s and 30s when they conjured up Arpanet.
